todaysEvents.summary: US blockade in effect second day; Pentagon: 'no ships made it past' blockade (6 Iran-bound tankers turned around), but 3 non-Iranian-bound tankers (Rich Starry, Elpis, Peace Gulf) transit Hormuz unimpeded (Al Jazeera, LSEG/Kpler data, Apr 14-15).
todaysEvents.delta: Blockade scope narrower than feared; commercial Hormuz transit partially preserved

todaysEvents.summary: Trump hints US-Iran talks could resume 'over next two days' in Islamabad; White House confirms second round under discussion; Iran delegation keeping Fri-Sun open (NBC, CNN, TIME Apr 14); 2-week ceasefire expires Apr 21 (Apr 15).
todaysEvents.delta: Diplomatic off-ramp re-emerges; tail risk reprices lower

todaysEvents.summary: Brent continues lower: $94.79 Apr 14 (CNBC) → ~$93 Apr 15; WTI $90.92 confirmed (TradingEconomics); US to let Iran oil sanctions waiver expire (Bloomberg); Europe plans postwar Hormuz coalition excluding US/Israel/Iran (Macron/WSJ, Apr 15).
todaysEvents.delta: Risk premium unwinds on talks optimism despite active blockade

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Second round of US-Iran Islamabad talks resumes within days; blockade serves as negotiating leverage rather than trigger for direct confrontation. Framework agreement on nuclear commitment + partial Hormuz sovereignty emerges before Apr 21 ceasefire expiry. P&I restoration phased over 2-3 weeks; Brent retraces toward $85-90/bbl as risk premium unwinds; iTraxx Asia IG tightens below 165bp. Netanyahu-Lebanon track provides parallel de-escalation channel.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: US blockade and Iran-bound port closure persist for 2-4 weeks; commercial Hormuz transit for non-Iranian flows continues but with elevated insurance costs. Second round of talks produces incremental progress but no framework before Apr 21 ceasefire expiry. Iran oil waiver expiration tightens sanctions enforcement. Brent stabilises $90-105/bbl; JKM $19-22/MMBtu; iTraxx Asia IG 170-195bp. IRGC conducts asymmetric harassment without triggering full kinetic engagement.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Second round of talks collapses; ceasefire lapses Apr 21 without extension. IRGC executes 'regretful response' - attacks US warships or commercial vessels transiting under US escort. Full ceasefire collapse; resumed US-Israel air campaign. Kharg Island seizure or Saudi Aramco attack brings GCC production under acute threat. Brent spikes $130-175/bbl; LNG markets seize; ASEAN recession risk becomes acute. Gold surges past $5,000/oz.
